initiative_name: 3.10.2 Energy transmission infrastructure — ii. Projections of network expansion requirements at least until 2040 (including for the year 2030)
parent_statement: Terna's Development Plan 2017 identifies priority development actions including overseas connections (HVDC Grand'Ile–Piossasco and Italy–Montenegro) and domestic grid projects to reduce congestion, increase safety/reliability in metropolitan areas, and improve quality and safety.
coverage_assessment: The sub-actions align well with the parent’s stated requirements: they target congestion reduction between market areas, increased safety/reliability in metro areas, and improved quality/safety, with multiple references to relieving constraints that limit renewable generation (South, Calabria, Sicily, Valle del Piave). However, the parent also highlights two overseas HVDC connections, but no sub-action here specifies those interconnectors, so coverage of the full parent scope is incomplete.
coherence_assessment: The sub-actions are largely complementary: they cover multiple bottlenecks across North/Central/South/Islands and include both new high-voltage lines and network streamlining/redevelopment. There is little redundancy, but the set reads as a portfolio list rather than a sequenced plan (no prioritization, dependencies, or timing).
weak_signals: No explicit deadlines/years, no quantified outcomes (MW/km/%/capacity), portfolio list without prioritization or milestones
strong_signals: Multiple named grid build/redevelopment projects (400 kV/150 kV/132 kV lines), explicit congestion-reduction and reliability objectives, multiple links to enabling renewable generation and cross-border exchanges
initiative_reasoning: The initiative aims to deliver a portfolio of priority electricity transmission projects to reduce congestion, improve safety/reliability and quality of service, and relieve constraints that limit renewable generation and inter-area/interconnection exchanges. Sub-action strength tally: 0 strong, 12 moderate, 0 weak—each is a concrete infrastructure intervention, but none includes an explicit measurable outcome target or a deadline/year in the provided verbatim. The parent text provides rationale (congestion/safety/quality) but does not establish a binding mechanism (e.g., mandate/regulation) or an explicit spatial scope like “nationwide by law,” so weaker measurability is not lifted by inheritance. Coverage is fairly high because the projects map to the parent’s stated needs across multiple regions, but it is incomplete because the parent mentions two overseas HVDC connections and these are not itemized among the sub-actions. Coherence is good as a complementary set of reinforcements, though it lacks sequencing and milestone structure. Applying the initiative soundness rule: it fails SOUND due to aggregate_measurability='No' and no 'strong' sub-actions; it meets PARTIAL because coverage_score>=0.4 and the cluster is clearly not aspirational (it specifies tangible build/redevelopment actions), even though it lacks quantified targets and timelines. Therefore the verdict is PARTIAL, with moderate confidence given the clear concreteness but weak trackability.
